questions.question: Which of the following is a common type of image degradation model?
questions.explanation: A Poisson noise model is a common type of image degradation model. It is often used to simulate the effect of photon shot noise in image formation.

questions.question: What is the term for the process of reconstructing an image from its degraded or distorted version?
questions.explanation: Image restoration is the term for the process of reconstructing an image from its degraded or distorted version.

questions.question: Which of the following methods is commonly used for image deblurring?
questions.explanation: The Wiener filter is a commonly used method for image deblurring. It is an adaptive filter that uses the power spectrum of the image to estimate the optimal filter.

questions.question: What is the term for the amount of information lost when an image is degraded?
questions.explanation: Image fidelity is the term for the amount of information lost when an image is degraded. It is a measure of the quality of the image.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key assumption in the model of image degradation/restoration process?
options: A) The image is a 2D array of pixels
B) The degradation process is linear
C) The restoration process is reversible
questions.explanation: The degradation process is often assumed to be linear, and the restoration process is also assumed to be linear. These assumptions are used to simplify the mathematical modeling of the image degradation/restoration process.
